---
id: DOC_R009
type: rule
name: principle_triple_layer_sync
display_name: "Principle Triple-Layer Synchronization"
implements: DOC_P001, DOC_R007
scope: "All principle creation, modification, and deletion operations"
---

# DOC_R009: Principle Triple-Layer Synchronization

## One-Liner

When any principle is added, modified, or deleted, ALL THREE documentation layers MUST be updated in a single operation: `.qmd` (source of truth) → `llm/*.yaml` (AI-optimized) → `.claude/rules/*.md` (quick reference).

## Rationale

The principles system serves three distinct consumers through three layers:

| Layer | Format | Consumer | Purpose |
|-------|--------|----------|---------|
| `.qmd` files | Full Quarto documents | Developers reading docs | Complete specification with rationale, examples, edge cases |
| `llm/*.yaml` | Structured YAML | AI agents (LLM context) | Machine-parseable rules with `rule_natural`, `rule_formal`, hierarchy |
| `.claude/rules/*.md` | Brief markdown | AI agents (auto-loaded) | Operational quick reference, loaded into every conversation |

If any layer falls out of sync, the consequences are:

- **Missing from `.qmd`**: The principle doesn't officially exist — no rationale, no authority
- **Missing from `llm/*.yaml`**: AI agents following the `llm/index.yaml` → chapter file workflow won't discover the rule; `principle_count` becomes wrong
- **Missing from `.claude/rules/*.md`**: AI agents in conversation won't be reminded of the rule in their auto-loaded context

The system has experienced this drift before: principles existed in `.qmd` but were absent from `llm/` YAML, causing AI agents to unknowingly violate rules they couldn't see. This rule prevents that class of error.

## Rule

### Trigger

This rule applies whenever:

1. A **new principle** is created (any MP, P, or R)
2. An **existing principle** is modified (id, name, scope, rule content)
3. A principle is **deleted or deprecated**
4. A principle's **relationships** change (implements, related)

### Three Layers and Their Roles

```
Layer 1: .qmd (Source of Truth)
  Location: docs/en/part1_principles/{CHAPTER}/rules/{ID}_{name}.qmd
  Content: Full specification — YAML frontmatter, rationale, rule details,
           examples (compliant + violation), relationships
  Authority: HIGHEST — all other layers derive from this

        │
        ▼ (extract structured summary)

Layer 2: llm/*.yaml (AI-Optimized Index)
  Location: llm/{chapter}.yaml
  Content: id, type, name, display_name, one_liner, implements, scope,
           file_path, rule_natural, rule_formal, violations[], compliant[]
  Updates: principle_count, hierarchy section
  Authority: DERIVED — must match .qmd

        │
        ▼ (extract operational brief)

Layer 3: .claude/rules/*.md (Quick Reference)
  Location: .claude/rules/{NN}-{topic}.md
  Content: 2-4 line summary + "Details: {path to .qmd}"
  Authority: DERIVED — must match .qmd
  Constraint: DOC_R007 (max 15 lines per section)
```

### Required Updates Per Layer

#### Layer 1: `.qmd` File

- Create or edit the `.qmd` file with full content
- YAML frontmatter MUST include: `id`, `type`, `name`, `display_name`, `implements`, `scope`
- Body MUST include: `# {ID}: {Display Name}`, `## One-Liner`, `## Rationale`, `## Rule`, `## Relationship`, `## Examples`

#### Layer 2: `llm/*.yaml` File

- Add or update the principle entry in the correct chapter YAML
- Update `principle_count` at the top of the file
- Add or update the entry in the `hierarchy:` section at the bottom
- Ensure `file_path` points to the correct `.qmd` relative path

#### Layer 3: `.claude/rules/*.md` File

- Add a brief section (2-4 lines) in the appropriate rules file
- Include `**Details**: {path to .qmd}` reference link
- If the principle is important enough for the "相關原則" list at the bottom, add it there too
- Follow DOC_R007 brevity constraints

### Execution Order

The layers MUST be updated in this order:

1. **`.qmd` first** — establish the authoritative content
2. **`llm/*.yaml` second** — extract structured summary from `.qmd`
3. **`.claude/rules/*.md` third** — extract operational brief

This order ensures each derived layer has its source available when being written.

### Verification Checklist

After completing all three updates, verify:

- [ ] `.qmd` file exists at the correct path with complete content
- [ ] `llm/*.yaml` has the new entry with matching `id`, `one_liner`, `implements`
- [ ] `llm/*.yaml` `principle_count` is correct (count all entries)
- [ ] `llm/*.yaml` `hierarchy:` section includes the new principle
- [ ] `.claude/rules/*.md` has a brief reference with `Details:` link
- [ ] All three layers agree on `id`, `name`, `scope`, and `implements`

### PostToolUse Hook Support

The `check-principle-sync.sh` PostToolUse hook detects modifications to ANY of the three layers and reminds about updating the others:

- **`.qmd` edited** → reminds to update `llm/*.yaml` + `.claude/rules/*.md` (with specific file suggestions)
- **`llm/*.yaml` edited** → reminds to verify `.qmd` source exists and update `.claude/rules/*.md`
- **`.claude/rules/*.md` edited** → reminds to verify `.qmd` and `llm/*.yaml` are already updated

The hook is advisory (non-blocking) and registered in `.claude/settings.json` as a PostToolUse hook on `Edit|Write`.

## Relationship

- **Implements**: DOC_P001 (Documentation System Architecture — ensures documentation layers stay consistent)
- **Implements**: DOC_R007 (Claude Rules Writing Convention — Layer 3 follows DOC_R007 brevity rules)
- **Related**: All MP/P/R principles (this rule governs how they are documented)
- **Enforced by**: PostToolUse hook `check-principle-sync.sh` (advisory reminder)

## Examples

### Compliant

- Created `DOC_R008.qmd` → added DOC_R008 to `CH19_documentation.yaml` (entry + principle_count + hierarchy) → added 4-line summary to `05-documentation.md`
- Modified `DM_R028` scope in `.qmd` → updated `rule_natural` in `CH02_data.yaml` → updated brief in `02-coding.md`
- Deleted deprecated `DEV_R010` → removed from `CH03_development.yaml` (updated count and hierarchy) → removed from `02-coding.md`

### Violation

- Created `.qmd` but forgot to update `llm/*.yaml` — AI agents can't discover the rule through the standard workflow
- Updated `llm/*.yaml` but not `.qmd` — the YAML content has no authoritative source, may drift
- Updated `.qmd` and `llm/*.yaml` but not `.claude/rules/*.md` — conversational AI agents miss the rule in auto-loaded context
- Updated `principle_count` but forgot to add to `hierarchy:` section — hierarchy becomes incomplete
- Added principle to wrong chapter YAML (e.g., UI rule in CH02_data.yaml) — agents looking in the correct chapter won't find it
